review: I ordered two sets as Christmas gifts and they were perfect. The hand-lettering is so beautiful - each card feels like it was made just for you. The linen ribbon packaging made them gift-ready right out of the box. Will absolutely order again!

These Advent cards are stunning. I used them on our family dinner table for each week of Advent - the Hope, Peace, Joy, and Love themes were exactly right. The botanical linework is delicate and the cardstock feels premium. Already planning to order next year's set in October so I don't miss out.

Bought two bookmark sets - one for me and one as a gift - and they are just lovely. The pressed zinnias are vivid even under the laminate and the tassels add a nice finish. The seller included a sweet handwritten note, which made me smile. This is what Etsy is for.

I commissioned a custom name print for a friend's ordination and Rebecca delivered beyond what I hoped for. She reached out to ask clarifying questions, chose a scripture that was meaningful and specific, and the lettering was breathtaking. The 8x10 is now framed and hanging in his study. This is a gift people keep forever.

Downloaded the mug wrap template and printed it at my local copy shop on sublimation paper. It came out beautifully. The instructions were clear and the terracotta colorway is warm and inviting. Such a lovely DIY gift idea for under five dollars. Thank you!

I'm a hospice nurse and I keep a stack of Rebecca's sympathy cards on my desk. They say what I often struggle to say - that grief is real and love is worth it. Every family I give one to reads it and tears up in a good way. I've bought three different times now. Quality is consistent and shipping is always prompt.

The bird feeder is adorable and sturdy - it's been up in my yard for three weeks and the cardinals found it immediately. The zinnia paint design adds cheerful color to the garden bed where it lives. Shipping was careful; arrived wrapped in tissue paper. Rebecca even included a little note about which birds prefer which seed. That's the touch that sets a shop apart.

I've been using the prayer journal every morning for six weeks and it has genuinely changed my devotional practice. The prompts move you through gratitude, lament, listening, and intention - that's a full conversation with God in twenty minutes. The linen cover has held up beautifully and the ribbon marker stays put. Already ordered a second one as a gift.

Ordered birthday cards with my sister's name added and they turned out so well. The watercolor zinnia wreath is cheerful and the sentiment - 'You carry more grace than you know' - is exactly what she needed to hear. Personalization was handled quickly and the final product was even prettier than the listing photo.

The devotional planner pages are well-designed and printer-friendly. I use the A5 version in my binder and the letter size for my desk calendar. The evening reflection prompt is my favorite - it closes the day well. At $7 for unlimited prints this is one of the best purchases I've made on Etsy in years.

These pot markers are charming and the sayings are perfect - 'Bloom Anyway' is my favorite. The kiln-fired finish is sturdy and has survived our spring rain. Giving 4 stars because one arrived with a hairline crack in the stake, but it still stands firm. Seller responded quickly when I mentioned it and offered a replacement. Great customer care.

The herb bundles are fragrant, full, and beautifully tied. I hung one in my kitchen and one in my linen closet - both smell wonderful. Knowing they came from someone's actual backyard garden in Memphis makes them feel like a real gift rather than a product. Rebecca even noted which week she harvested them. Lovely.
